semantic.role: Build orchestration script for the ArchSpine project (TypeScript compilation, resource copying, and mock cleanup).
responsibilities: Cleans and rebuilds the dist/ directory from scratch
Runs the TypeScript compiler (tsc) to produce compiled JavaScript
Copies static rule files from src/ast/rules to dist/ast/rules
Recursively copies src/assets to dist/assets (excluding .ts files and __mocks__ directories)
Removes all __mocks__ directories from the dist/ tree after build
Makes the CLI entry point (dist/cli/index.js) executable
outOfScope: TypeScript configuration or error handling beyond build
Runtime behavior of the built application

English.purpose: This document (a build script) ensures that every developer and CI pipeline produces a consistent, production-ready dist/ output for the ArchSpine mirror system. It eliminates stale artifacts, enforces coverage of critical files (rules and assets), and prevents mock data from leaking into the final build.
English.context_and_audience: Written for developers who maintain the ArchSpine build process, CI/CD engineers, and anyone modifying the project’s compilation steps. The script assumes the repository is already installed and dependencies available, and it runs as part of pre-publish or CI steps.
key_takeaways: The build resets dist/, compiles TypeScript, then copies non-`.ts` resources from `src/ast/rules` and `src/assets`.
Mock directories (`__mocks__`) are explicitly excluded during copy and removed recursively after build to prevent testing artifacts from appearing in the distribution.
The CLI entry point is given execute permission automatically.
Customization points: `shouldExcludeDistEntry()` allows extending exclusion logic for other directory names or patterns.
